key_insight: The paper accelerates phase-field cohesive zone modeling to simulate mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue of UHPC and HSS efficiently, without resolving every load cycle.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical computational tool for assessing durability and crack growth in cyclic-loaded infrastructure made from high-performance materials.
method: Derive a generic stress-based failure criterion for UHPC from biaxial tensile tests.
Embed a fatigue degradation function into the PF-CZM framework.
Use an envelope-load acceleration scheme with adaptive cyclic increments to capture the three-stage fatigue process.
evidence_signal: The model is validated against relevant experimental comparisons and is reported to reproduce both mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue behavior of UHPC and HSS.
watch_item: Accuracy may depend on the chosen failure criterion, fatigue degradation law, and adaptive acceleration settings, so calibration to material-specific test data is important.
paper_title: High performance construction materials fracture and high cycle fatigue assessment based on accelerated PF-CZM
